mapping.a_element: Josephson junction coupled to a single-mode resonant cavity
mapping.b_element: multi-qubit system coupled to an optical cavity mode

mapping.a_element: gate voltage tuned to resonant absorption of photons (single- or two-photon processes)
mapping.b_element: critical coupling strength triggering a super-radiant phase transition

mapping.a_element: simplified two-level Hamiltonian approximation near resonance
mapping.b_element: collective behavior of qubits leading to a phase transition (e.g., Dicke model)

mapping.a_element: collapse and revival phenomenon under initial conditions

brief_justification: While both papers involve a quantum system (junction/qubits) coupled to a cavity, Paper A focuses on resonant photon absorption and eigenstate entanglement in a *single-junction* system, whereas Paper B describes a *collective phase transition* in a *multi-qubit* system. The former is a quantum dynamical process (collapse/revival), while the latter is a thermodynamic phase transition, driven by distinct underlying mechanisms.
